content:
You are a senior site reliability engineer. Analyze the following server logs from a production incident that occurred between 03:42 and 04:15 UTC. Identify the root cause, the cascade failure sequence, and write a detailed post-mortem with timeline, impact assessment, and remediation plan.

Server logs:
```
2026-04-17T03:42:01.123Z [INFO]  web-01 nginx: upstream response time 0.142s for /api/v2/inference
2026-04-17T03:42:03.445Z [INFO]  web-01 nginx: upstream response time 0.189s for /api/v2/inference
2026-04-17T03:42:11.002Z [WARN]  gpu-01 vllm: KV cache utilization at 78% (threshold: 80%)
2026-04-17T03:42:15.334Z [INFO]  web-02 nginx: upstream response time 0.201s for /api/v2/inference
2026-04-17T03:42:22.891Z [WARN]  gpu-01 vllm: KV cache utilization at 82% — preemption starting
2026-04-17T03:42:23.001Z [INFO]  gpu-01 vllm: preempting request req-4829 (priority: low, tokens: 12847)
2026-04-17T03:42:23.445Z [INFO]  gpu-01 vllm: preempting request req-4831 (priority: low, tokens: 9234)
2026-04-17T03:42:25.112Z [WARN]  gpu-02 vllm: KV cache utilization at 79%
2026-04-17T03:42:30.002Z [ERROR] lb-01 haproxy: backend gpu-01 health check failed (timeout 5000ms)
2026-04-17T03:42:30.223Z [WARN]  lb-01 haproxy: removing gpu-01 from pool (active connections: 47)
2026-04-17T03:42:30.889Z [INFO]  lb-01 haproxy: redistributing 47 connections to gpu-02, gpu-03
2026-04-17T03:42:31.002Z [WARN]  gpu-02 vllm: KV cache utilization at 91% — preemption starting
2026-04-17T03:42:31.445Z [WARN]  gpu-03 vllm: KV cache utilization at 85% — preemption starting
2026-04-17T03:42:32.001Z [ERROR] gpu-02 vllm: OOM in KV cache — dropping batch of 23 requests
2026-04-17T03:42:32.334Z [ERROR] gpu-03 vllm: OOM in KV cache — dropping batch of 18 requests
2026-04-17T03:42:33.001Z [ERROR] lb-01 haproxy: backend gpu-02 health check failed
2026-04-17T03:42:33.223Z [ERROR] lb-01 haproxy: backend gpu-03 health check failed
2026-04-17T03:42:33.445Z [CRITICAL] lb-01 haproxy: NO HEALTHY BACKENDS — service unavailable
2026-04-17T03:42:33.890Z [ERROR] web-01 nginx: 502 Bad Gateway for 100% of requests
2026-04-17T03:42:34.112Z [ERROR] web-02 nginx: 502 Bad Gateway for 100% of requests
2026-04-17T03:42:34.334Z [ALERT] pagerduty: P1 incident created — inference service down (incident-7821)
2026-04-17T03:43:01.001Z [INFO]  gpu-01 vllm: health check passing — KV cache at 45% after preemption
2026-04-17T03:43:01.223Z [INFO]  lb-01 haproxy: adding gpu-01 back to pool
2026-04-17T03:43:02.001Z [INFO]  gpu-02 vllm: restarting after OOM crash
2026-04-17T03:43:02.334Z [INFO]  gpu-03 vllm: restarting after OOM crash
2026-04-17T03:43:15.001Z [INFO]  lb-01 haproxy: gpu-01 serving 100% of traffic (load: 94%)
2026-04-17T03:43:22.001Z [WARN]  gpu-01 vllm: KV cache utilization at 83% — preemption starting
2026-04-17T03:43:23.001Z [ERROR] lb-01 haproxy: backend gpu-01 health check failed again
2026-04-17T03:43:23.334Z [CRITICAL] lb-01 haproxy: NO HEALTHY BACKENDS — service unavailable (second outage)
2026-04-17T03:44:01.001Z [INFO]  on-call-eng: manual intervention — enabling request throttling at lb-01
2026-04-17T03:44:05.001Z [INFO]  lb-01 haproxy: rate limiting to 200 req/s (down from 1200 req/s)
2026-04-17T03:44:10.001Z [INFO]  gpu-02 vllm: model loaded, joining pool
2026-04-17T03:44:12.001Z [INFO]  gpu-03 vllm: model loaded, joining pool
2026-04-17T03:44:15.001Z [INFO]  lb-01 haproxy: 3 healthy backends, distributing load
2026-04-17T03:44:20.001Z [INFO]  gpu-01 vllm: KV cache at 61%, stable
2026-04-17T03:44:20.334Z [INFO]  gpu-02 vllm: KV cache at 58%, stable
2026-04-17T03:44:20.445Z [INFO]  gpu-03 vllm: KV cache at 55%, stable
2026-04-17T03:44:45.001Z [INFO]  lb-01 haproxy: gradually increasing rate limit to 400 req/s
2026-04-17T03:45:30.001Z [INFO]  lb-01 haproxy: rate limit at 600 req/s, all backends stable
2026-04-17T03:47:00.001Z [INFO]  lb-01 haproxy: rate limit at 900 req/s, KV cache stable at 70-72%
2026-04-17T03:50:00.001Z [INFO]  lb-01 haproxy: rate limit removed, operating normally at 1150 req/s
2026-04-17T03:50:05.001Z [INFO]  pagerduty: incident-7821 resolved after 7m 31s
2026-04-17T04:00:00.001Z [INFO]  monitoring: error rate back to baseline (0.02%)
```

Additional context:
- Peak traffic started at 03:40 UTC (promotional campaign began)
- Each GPU node has 80GB VRAM, models load at ~45GB
- Health check timeout is 5000ms, configured conservatively to avoid flapping
- The haproxy health check calls /health endpoint which blocks on vllm internal locks during preemption
- Normal operating throughput: 400-600 req/s per GPU node
- The load balancer uses round-robin with no connection-count awareness
- Model restart time: approximately 70 seconds to reload weights
- There are 4 GPU nodes total but gpu-04 was offline for scheduled maintenance

Write:
1. Executive summary (3-4 sentences, non-technical audience)
2. Detailed incident timeline with annotations explaining what each event means technically
3. Root cause analysis — identify the PRIMARY root cause and up to 3 contributing factors
4. Cascade failure analysis — explain the mechanism by which gpu-01 failure caused total outage
5. Five-whys analysis for the primary root cause
6. Impact assessment: estimated requests dropped, duration, affected users
7. Immediate remediation actions taken and their effectiveness
8. 10-item action plan to prevent recurrence, categorized by: (a) immediate/this week, (b) short-term/this quarter, (c) long-term/architectural
9. Metrics and alerting gaps identified from the logs
10. What a graceful degradation response would have looked like instead

You are reviewing a distributed system for a real-time collaborative text editor (similar to Google Docs). The system uses Operational Transformation (OT) for conflict resolution. Below is the complete server-side implementation of the OT engine and WebSocket handler. Perform a thorough architectural review, identify correctness bugs, performance bottlenecks, and scalability limits.

```python
# ot_engine.py — Operational Transformation Engine
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Optional, Dict, Any, Tuple
import time
import asyncio
import json
import hashlib
from collections import defaultdict

@dataclass
class Operation:
    """A single OT operation."""
    op_type: str  # 'insert', 'delete', 'retain'
    position: int
    content: str = ''
    length: int = 0
    client_id: str = ''
    revision: int = 0
    timestamp: float = field(default_factory=time.time)

@dataclass
class Document:
    """Server-side document state."""
    doc_id: str
    content: str = ''
    revision: int = 0
    history: List[Operation] = field(default_factory=list)
    clients: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    lock: asyncio.Lock = field(default_factory=asyncio.Lock)

class OTEngine:
    def __init__(self):
        self.documents: Dict[str, Document] = {}
        
    def get_or_create_document(self, doc_id: str) -> Document:
        if doc_id not in self.documents:
            self.documents[doc_id] = Document(doc_id=doc_id)
        return self.documents[doc_id]
    
    def apply_operation(self, doc: Document, op: Operation) -> str:
        """Apply operation to document content."""
        content = doc.content
        if op.op_type == 'insert':
            new_content = content[:op.position] + op.content + content[op.position:]
        elif op.op_type == 'delete':
            new_content = content[:op.position] + content[op.position + op.length:]
        elif op.op_type == 'retain':
            new_content = content
        else:
            raise ValueError(f'Unknown op type: {op.op_type}')
        return new_content
    
    def transform(self, op1: Operation, op2: Operation) -> Tuple[Operation, Operation]:
        """Transform op1 against op2, return transformed versions."""
        op1_prime = Operation(**vars(op1))
        op2_prime = Operation(**vars(op2))
        
        if op1.op_type == 'insert' and op2.op_type == 'insert':
            if op1.position <= op2.position:
                op2_prime.position += len(op1.content)
            else:
                op1_prime.position += len(op2.content)
                
        elif op1.op_type == 'insert' and op2.op_type == 'delete':
            if op1.position <= op2.position:
                op2_prime.position += len(op1.content)
            elif op1.position > op2.position + op2.length:
                op1_prime.position -= op2.length
            else:
                op1_prime.position = op2.position
                
        elif op1.op_type == 'delete' and op2.op_type == 'insert':
            if op2.position <= op1.position:
                op1_prime.position += len(op2.content)
            elif op2.position > op1.position + op1.length:
                op2_prime.position -= op1.length
            else:
                op2_prime.position = op1.position
                
        elif op1.op_type == 'delete' and op2.op_type == 'delete':
            if op1.position + op1.length <= op2.position:
                op2_prime.position -= op1.length
            elif op2.position + op2.length <= op1.position:
                op1_prime.position -= op2.length
            else:
                # Overlapping deletes - handle overlap
                overlap_start = max(op1.position, op2.position)
                overlap_end = min(op1.position + op1.length, op2.position + op2.length)
                overlap = overlap_end - overlap_start
                op1_prime.length = max(0, op1.length - overlap)
                op2_prime.length = max(0, op2.length - overlap)
                
        return op1_prime, op2_prime
    
    async def submit_operation(self, doc_id: str, op: Operation) -> Dict:
        """Submit an operation from a client."""
        doc = self.get_or_create_document(doc_id)
        
        async with doc.lock:
            # Transform against all operations since client's revision
            ops_since = doc.history[op.revision:]
            
            current_op = op
            for hist_op in ops_since:
                current_op, _ = self.transform(current_op, hist_op)
            
            # Apply the transformed operation
            doc.content = self.apply_operation(doc, current_op)
            doc.revision += 1
            current_op.revision = doc.revision
            doc.history.append(current_op)
            
            return {
                'success': True,
                'revision': doc.revision,
                'operation': vars(current_op),
                'checksum': hashlib.md5(doc.content.encode()).hexdigest()
            }

# websocket_handler.py
import websockets
import asyncio
from typing import Set

engine = OTEngine()
connected_clients: Dict[str, Set] = defaultdict(set)

async def handle_client(websocket, path):
    doc_id = path.strip('/')
    doc = engine.get_or_create_document(doc_id)
    connected_clients[doc_id].add(websocket)
    doc.clients[websocket] = {'joined_at': time.time()}
    
    try:
        # Send current document state
        await websocket.send(json.dumps({
            'type': 'init',
            'content': doc.content,
            'revision': doc.revision
        }))
        
        async for message in websocket:
            data = json.loads(message)
            
            if data['type'] == 'operation':
                op = Operation(
                    op_type=data['op_type'],
                    position=data['position'],
                    content=data.get('content', ''),
                    length=data.get('length', 0),
                    client_id=data['client_id'],
                    revision=data['revision']
                )
                
                result = await engine.submit_operation(doc_id, op)
                
                # Broadcast to all clients
                broadcast_msg = json.dumps({
                    'type': 'operation',
                    'operation': result['operation'],
                    'revision': result['revision'],
                    'checksum': result['checksum']
                })
                
                disconnected = set()
                for client in connected_clients[doc_id]:
                    try:
                        await client.send(broadcast_msg)
                    except websockets.exceptions.ConnectionClosed:
                        disconnected.add(client)
                
                for client in disconnected:
                    connected_clients[doc_id].discard(client)
                    
    except websockets.exceptions.ConnectionClosed:
        pass
    finally:
        connected_clients[doc_id].discard(websocket)
        del doc.clients[websocket]

start_server = websockets.serve(handle_client, 'localhost', 8765)

asio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(start_server)
asyncio.get_event_loop().run_forever()
```

Provide:
1. Correctness analysis: identify at least 5 OT algorithm bugs that would cause document divergence
2. Concurrency analysis: identify race conditions and lock-ordering problems
3. Memory and scalability analysis: what breaks at 1000 concurrent documents? 100k?
4. Security analysis: what can a malicious client do?
5. Performance bottlenecks: where is the O(n) or O(n²) complexity hiding?
6. Missing features that would be required for production (list at least 8)
7. Alternative approaches: when would you choose CRDT (e.g., Yjs, Automerge) over OT, and vice versa?
8. A corrected implementation of the transform() function for the delete-delete case
9. Architecture diagram description for a production-grade version supporting 100k simultaneous editors across 5 regions
10. Estimated engineering effort to make this production-ready, broken down by component

You are a technical architect. Write a comprehensive technical specification for a new feature: real-time collaborative AI prompt engineering workspace. This is a web application where multiple users can simultaneously edit, version, test, and optimize LLM prompts together.

Feature requirements gathered from user research:
- Up to 50 simultaneous collaborators per workspace
- Real-time collaborative editing of prompt templates with variable substitution syntax like {{variable_name}}
- Version history with branching (like git branches, not just linear history)
- Side-by-side A/B testing of two prompt variants against the same test cases
- Automated evaluation: run prompts against a test suite and score outputs using LLM-as-judge
- Prompt chaining: connect multiple prompts where output of one feeds into another
- Model comparison: run the same prompt against multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, local models)
- Role-based access: Owner, Editor, Commenter, Viewer
- Real-time presence indicators (who is editing what)
- Comment threads anchored to specific positions in the prompt text
- Export to Python/TypeScript SDK code
- Webhook notifications when evaluation scores drop below threshold
- Prompt marketplace: share prompts publicly with usage analytics

Deliverable: Write the technical specification including:

1. **System overview** — high-level architecture with component descriptions
2. **Data model** — complete entity definitions with fields, types, relationships, and constraints for: Workspace, PromptTemplate, PromptVersion, PromptBranch, Variable, TestCase, EvaluationRun, EvaluationResult, ModelProvider, PromptChain, Comment, User, Permission
3. **API design** — RESTful endpoints for all CRUD operations plus WebSocket events for real-time collaboration. Include request/response shapes for the 5 most complex endpoints.
4. **Collaborative editing protocol** — how simultaneous edits are handled (OT vs CRDT decision with justification), conflict resolution, presence protocol
5. **Evaluation pipeline** — architecture for running async evaluations, LLM-as-judge implementation, scoring rubrics, comparison statistics
6. **Branching model** — how prompt version branching works (data model, merge semantics, conflict detection)
7. **Prompt chaining execution** — DAG representation, execution order, variable passing, error handling, circular dependency detection
8. **Performance requirements** — latency targets, throughput requirements, caching strategy
9. **Security model** — authentication, authorization matrix, prompt injection risks in the evaluation pipeline
10. **Implementation roadmap** — phased delivery plan across 4 quarters with team size recommendations and dependency ordering
11. **Open questions and risks** — at least 8 unresolved design decisions with trade-off analysis
12. **Competitive analysis** — compare to PromptLayer, Langsmith, Promptfoo, and Helicone; identify where this spec is differentiated
